unit.name: Self-attention, I3D inflation, modern video transformers, and downstream video tasks
unit.description: Covers nonlocal self-attention blocks, I3D transfer from image networks, transformer progress, video-model visualization, and localization/detection tasks.
unit.summary: Because RNNs are hard to parallelize, the lecture turns to self-attention, whose query-key-value alignment can be computed in parallel and extended to video feature maps [ts=3102s] [ts=3128s] [ts=3141s]. A spatio-temporal self-attention or nonlocal block starts from C x T x H x W features, uses 1 x 1 x 1 convolutions to form query, key, and value feature maps, computes attention over query-key pairs, conditions the values, maps back to C, and adds a residual connection [ts=3149s] [ts=3170s] [ts=3201s]. Such nonlocal blocks can be inserted into 3D CNNs to fuse information across both space and time [ts=3221s] [ts=3252s]. The I3D idea asks whether successful 2D image architectures can be reused for video by replacing each 2D K_h x K_w convolution or pooling layer with a 3D K_t x K_h x K_w version, and even copying pretrained 2D weights K_t times while dividing by K_t [ts=3270s] [ts=3295s] [ts=3363s]. This inflation preserves output for a constant-frame video and gives useful image-pretrained initialization for fine-tuning video models [ts=3382s] [ts=3402s] [ts=3485s]. The segment then surveys video transformers, including factorized space-time attention, token pooling, and masked autoencoder pretraining, noting large progress on Kinetics-400 up to around 90% top-1 accuracy [ts=3500s] [ts=3526s] [ts=3550s]. Finally, it describes gradient-ascent visualization of two-stream video models and moves from clip classification to temporal action localization and spatio-temporal detection, where actions must be localized in time and possibly in both space and time [ts=3579s] [ts=3655s] [ts=3676s] [ts=3723s].

key_points.text: Self-attention is attractive for video because query-key-value alignment can be computed in parallel.
A nonlocal video block forms query, key, and value maps from C x T x H x W features using 1 x 1 x 1 3D convolutions.
Nonlocal blocks can be inserted into 3D CNNs to fuse across both space and time.
I3D inflates image CNNs by replacing each 2D K_h x K_w operation with a 3D K_t x K_h x K_w operation.
Inflated 3D kernels can be initialized by copying a 2D kernel K_t times and dividing by K_t.
Modern video transformers use ideas such as factorized space-time attention, token pooling, and masked autoencoder pretraining.
Video models can be visualized by gradient ascent on appearance and flow inputs to maximize a class score.
Temporal action localization identifies when actions occur; spatio-temporal detection localizes activities in both space and time.

kp_catalog.name: Spatio-temporal self-attention and nonlocal blocks
kp_catalog.description: Nonlocal blocks extend self-attention to C x T x H x W video feature maps using query, key, and value projections.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because spatio-temporal self-attention and nonlocal blocks formalizes how nonlocal blocks extend self-attention to C x T x H x W video feature maps using query, key, and value projections, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: I3D inflation and weight transfer
kp_catalog.description: I3D converts 2D image networks to video by inflating kernels and pooling layers to 3D and reusing pretrained image weights.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because i3D inflation and weight transfer clarifies i3D converts 2D image networks to video by inflating kernels and pooling layers to 3D and reusing pretrained image weights, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Video transformers and masked autoencoders
kp_catalog.description: Modern video models use factorized attention, token pooling, and masked-autoencoder pretraining for scalable video understanding.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because video transformers and masked autoencoders clarifies modern video models use factorized attention, token pooling, and masked-autoencoder pretraining for scalable video understanding, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Temporal localization and spatio-temporal detection
kp_catalog.description: Beyond clip classification, video understanding can localize actions in time and detect people/actions jointly in space and time.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because temporal localization and spatio-temporal detection synthesizes beyond clip classification, video understanding can localize actions in time and detect people/actions jointly in space and time into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
